description: A sandwich made with slow-cooked pork shoulder that is shredded and served on a bun, often with barbecue sauce and coleslaw. It is especially associated with American barbecue traditions.
preparation_steps: Season and slow-cook pork shoulder until very tender.
Shred the meat and mix with some of its cooking juices and, if desired, barbecue sauce.
Pile the pulled pork onto the bun and add optional toppings such as coleslaw or pickles.

health_advice.benefits: Provides substantial protein and can be filling.
health_advice.risks: Calories, sodium, and sugar can be high depending on the cut of pork, sauce, and portion size.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use a leaner portion of pork, moderate the sauce, and add a crisp vegetable topping such as slaw without excessive dressing.

dietary_notes.note: Usually not vegetarian or vegan. Gluten-free is possible if served without a standard bun or with a certified gluten-free bun and sauce.
